title: Fix color picker: inline JSX instead of nested function component
body:
## Problem
The `ColorPicker` was defined as a named function inside `CompassControlPanel` and used as `<ColorPicker />`. React sees a new component type on every render, causing it to unmount and remount the entire picker on each state change. This broke:
- Color selection (state never persisted across the remount)
- Hover effects (elements destroyed and recreated mid-hover)

## Fix
Converted to a `const colorPicker = (...)` JSX variable rendered as `{colorPicker}`. Same closure access to all state and handlers, but stable DOM elements across renders.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Preview custom bearing on dial while in True North mode
body:
## Summary
- Bearing slider now updates the compass dial needle in real time even while in True North mode
- The device LEDs are still unchanged until the user taps "Custom" — only the web preview updates
- Implemented via an \`onTargetChange\` callback prop; \`Compass.tsx\` holds the dial target in local state rather than deriving it from BLE state

title: Fix bearing slider in manual mode and LED color in quest mode
body:
## Summary
- **Manual mode bearing slider** now correctly sends `setTarget` to the firmware — the early-return guard was scoped to all modes instead of just compass+North mode
- **Quest mode LED color** now uses `activeColor()` (user-selected color + random support) instead of the hardcoded `COLOR_NORTH` gold — custom bearing mode now respects the color picker

Note: the `activeColor()` fix is a firmware change in `compass.ino` that requires a flash to the device.

## Test plan
- [ ] Manual mode: drag bearing slider — lit LED should move in real time
- [ ] Custom bearing mode: change color preset — LED should reflect the selected color
- [ ] Random color toggle in custom mode — LED should cycle colors

title: Unify compass mode: shared controls, always-visible bearing, one-tap switch
body:
## Summary
- **Target Bearing slider is always visible** — dial it in while in True North mode, then tap once to activate
- **Replaced real/custom sub-mode buttons** with a single True North | Custom toggle pair
- **Bearing only sent to firmware when active** — adjusting the slider in North mode pre-sets the value locally without triggering an auto-switch to quest mode
- **All LED settings shared** — color, brightness, spread, spread intensity always visible regardless of which direction mode is active
- No firmware changes required

## Test plan
- [ ] In True North mode, drag bearing slider — LED ring should not change
- [ ] Press "Custom" button — ring should immediately snap to the pre-set bearing
- [ ] Drag slider while in Custom mode — ring should follow in real time  
- [ ] Press "True North" — ring returns to ambient North-tracking
- [ ] Color/brightness/spread changes apply in both modes

title: Fix compass dial: static rose, amber needle points to target
body:
## Summary
- N/E/S/W labels are now fixed (static rose — they do not rotate)
- The amber tip of the needle always points toward the target bearing on the fixed rose; in ambient mode (target = North = 0°) it stays pinned to the N label
- A small purple tick mark rotates with the current device heading, showing which direction the device is pointing against the fixed rose
- `useRef` accumulates an unbounded angle so the CSS transition always takes the short arc across the 0°/360° boundary — no more long spin-back animation

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect device in ambient mode — amber tip should be pinned to N, purple tick should move as device rotates
- [ ] Send a quest target (e.g. 90°) — amber tip should animate to E label
- [ ] Rotate device slowly past 360°/0° — needle should animate the short way, not spin backwards

title: Auto-return to compass mode after finishing calibration
body:
## Summary

- After pressing Finish on the magnetometer calibration, the web UI stayed on the Calibrate tab even though the firmware had already returned to ambient mode — leaving the sweep animation visible until the user manually tapped Compass
- Added `switchTopMode("compass")` at the end of `handleFinishMagCal` to automatically return, which also sends `compass.setMode: ambient` to the device ensuring the LED ring redraws immediately

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect device, go to Calibrate tab
- [ ] Start and complete magnetometer calibration
- [ ] After pressing Finish, UI should automatically switch back to Compass tab
- [ ] LED ring should stop sweeping and snap to North position

title: Fix compass needle direction: point toward North, not heading
body:
## Summary

- The compass dial needle previously rotated by `+heading`, making it a heading indicator (pointing where the device faces)
- The physical LED ring in ambient mode points toward geographic North — the web needle should be consistent
- Changed to `rotate(360 - heading)` so the needle always points toward North as the device rotates

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect compass device
- [ ] Face North — needle should point up (to N on the rose)
- [ ] Rotate 90° clockwise to face East — needle should swing left (North is now to your left)
- [ ] Rotate to face South — needle should point down (North is behind you)
- [ ] Needle direction should match the physical LED ring's behavior

title: Fix device showing as Unknown Relic
body:
## Summary
- Web Bluetooth does not always populate `device.name` from the BLE advertisement packet (OS/browser dependent)
- Fall back to a friendly name derived from `DeviceInfo.type` (read from the GATT characteristic after connection)
- "Magic Compass" → compass, "Magic Lantern" → lantern, "Fairy Stones" → fairy-stones

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect a compass device — should show "Magic Compass" instead of "Unknown Relic"
- [ ] Devices that do advertise a name should still use that name (no regression)

title: Compass calibration, spread intensity, brightness, spin trail, and UI polish
body:
## Summary

- **Two-step magnetometer calibration** — Step 1 rotates device through all orientations to collect hard-iron offsets (saved to NVS); Step 2 sets the north bearing offset (also saved to NVS). Both survive power cycles.
- **Spread intensity control** — slider to adjust per-neighbor brightness falloff; always visible regardless of spread level
- **Global LED brightness** — acts as a relative modifier on all active LEDs (primary + spread neighbors)
- **Spin comet trail** — spin now leaves a directional trail behind the LED rather than symmetric spread, eliminating artifacts when combined with pulse
- **Pulse affects all active LEDs** — all spread neighbors breathe in lockstep with the primary
- **Color picker moved to top** of mode-specific controls
- **Header connect state** — returns to "Connect Relic" after page refresh (removed auto-reconnect on mount)
- **Protocol additions** — `compass.startMagCal`, `compass.finishMagCal`, `compass.setSpreadIntensity`, `compass.setBrightness`; `"calibrate"` added to compass mode union

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect compass relic; verify brightness slider dims all LEDs (primary + spread) proportionally
- [ ] Set spread to 2–4; verify spread intensity slider changes neighbor brightness
- [ ] Enable spin; verify comet trail appears behind direction of travel only
- [ ] Enable spin + pulse together; verify no LED cutoff artifacts
- [ ] Open Calibrate tab; complete Step 1 (rotate device ~20s), confirm violet sweep on ring, press Done
- [ ] Complete Step 2 (point north, press Set North); confirm heading resets to 0°
- [ ] Power-cycle device; confirm calibration persists (heading stays correct, `calibrated: true` in state)
- [ ] Refresh browser; confirm header shows "Connect Relic" not "Connecting…"

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Compass north-offset calibration
body:
## Summary
- **Calibrate tab** now shows a step-by-step UI instead of immediately triggering calibration
- User holds device level, points it north, presses **Set North**
- Firmware records the raw heading as a north offset, saves it to NVS (persists across power cycles)
- All future heading readings are offset so LED 0 always corresponds to true north
- Calibration status (dot + label) reflects both the device's `calibrated` state and a local 4-second success flash

## Test plan
- [ ] Enter Calibrate tab — should show instructions and Set North button, no command sent yet
- [ ] Point device north, press Set North — success message appears, Serial shows "North offset saved: X°"
- [ ] Power cycle the device — Serial shows "North offset loaded: X°", compass points north correctly on boot
- [ ] Recalibrate from a different orientation — new offset overwrites the old one

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Move color picker to top of mode controls
body:
## Summary
- Color picker now appears at the top of the control list in both compass and manual modes, above direction/bearing/spread/spin/pulse.

title: Header returns to Connect state after page refresh
body:
## Summary
- Removed the page-load `autoReconnect` `useEffect` that was calling `setConnecting(true)` on mount, leaving the header stuck on "Connecting…" after a page refresh.
- The mid-session auto-reconnect (`gattserverdisconnected` → `handleDC` backoff loop) is unchanged and still works as before.

## Behaviour after this change
- Page refresh → header immediately shows **Connect Relic**
- Mid-session BLE drop → header shows **Connecting…** and retries silently in the background

title: Always show Spread Intensity slider
body:
## Summary
- Spread Intensity slider is now always visible in compass and manual modes, regardless of whether Spread is set to 0.

title: Add spread intensity slider (independent of brightness)
body:
## Summary
- **Spread Intensity slider** appears in compass and manual modes whenever spread > 0. Controls how bright spread-neighbor LEDs are (10–100%, default 50%). Hides when spread = 0 to avoid clutter.
- **Brightness is now truly independent of spread**: primary LED uses the brightness cap; spread neighbors derive from the full color at `spreadIntensity^n` per ring — unaffected by brightness.
- New BLE command: `compass.setSpreadIntensity { intensity: 0–100 }` (wired in protocol.ts and synced on reconnect).
- Firmware counterparts already merged to `TabletopRelics-Firmware/main` (NeoRing.h + compass.ino + Protocol.h).

## Test plan
- [ ] Set spread to 1–4; adjust Spread Intensity slider — neighbor LEDs should brighten/dim while primary LED stays constant
- [ ] Lower Brightness slider — primary LED dims; spread neighbor brightness should not change
- [ ] Set spread to 0 — Spread Intensity slider should disappear
- [ ] Disconnect and reconnect — both brightness and spread intensity should restore correctly
- [ ] Test in compass Real north, compass Custom, manual, spin, pulse, and spin-pulse modes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add LED brightness control to all modes
body:
## Summary
Adds a **Brightness** slider (0–100%) to the compass control panel, visible in all modes. It sets a global cap on all LED output — the firmware maps the percentage to NeoPixel's `setBrightness(0–255)`, which scales every colour channel before it reaches the LEDs.

**Placement:** between the LEDs toggle and the Mode selector so it's always accessible regardless of active mode.

**Default:** 78% — matches the new firmware startup default (raised from the old hardcoded 80/255 ≈ 31%).

## Firmware changes (committed to firmware `main`)
- `Protocol.h`: adds `CMD_COMPASS_SET_BRIGHTNESS`
- `NeoRing.h`: adds `setBrightness(uint8_t)` public method; raises startup default from 80 → 200
- `compass.ino`: adds global `ledBrightnessPct` state and handler for the new command

## Test plan
- [ ] Brightness slider changes LED intensity in real time across all modes
- [ ] Slider at 0% turns LEDs off (independent of the LEDs On/Off toggle)
- [ ] Slider at 100% shows full brightness
- [ ] Value is restored on reconnect (synced via `syncToDevice`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Rename spill → spread; fix spread levels 3-4 invisible bug
body:
## Summary
**Web app:** Renames the "Spill" label and state to "Spread" throughout `CompassControlPanel`. The BLE op name (`compass.setSpill`) is unchanged for now.

**Firmware (`NeoRing.h`):** Fixes a bug where spread levels 3 and 4 were completely invisible.

### Root cause
The falloff multiplier was `0.125` per step, so:
| Level | Old brightness | Visible? |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 12.5% | ✓ (barely) |
| 2 | 1.6% | ✓ (dim) |
| 3 | 0.2% | ✗ rounds to 0 in 8-bit |
| 4 | 0.02% | ✗ |

### Fix
Changed to `0.5×` per step — clean halving at each ring:
| Level | New brightness | Visible? |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 50% | ✓ |
| 2 | 25% | ✓ |
| 3 | 12.5% | ✓ |
| 4 | 6.25% | ✓ |

Fixed in `showPointWithSpill`, `showSpinStep`, `showPulseStep`, and `showSpinPulseStep`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Spread = 0: single LED
- [ ] Spread = 1: center + 1 neighbor each side at ~50%
- [ ] Spread = 2: adds another ring at ~25%
- [ ] Spread = 3: visibly wider (was invisible before)
- [ ] Spread = 4: widest (was invisible before)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Compass mode redesign: merge ambient+quest, add color/spill
body:
## Summary
- **Renames** the top-level "Ambient" mode to "Compass"
- **Compass mode** now has two sub-modes:
  - **Real north** — magnetometer tracks true north (same as old Ambient)
  - **Custom** — bearing slider appears; device points to the chosen heading (same as old Quest)
- **Adds** color picker (presets + random) and spill slider to Compass mode
- **Removes** Quest as a standalone top-level mode
- Color picker is a shared inline component reused by Compass and Manual modes

## Firmware
No firmware changes needed — the UI maps to existing `ambient` and `quest` firmware modes.

## Test plan
- [ ] Compass → Real north: device tracks north, color/spill controls work
- [ ] Compass → Custom: bearing slider appears, device points to selected heading
- [ ] Manual mode: unaffected, still has all spin/pulse/bearing/color controls
- [ ] Calibrate: unaffected
- [ ] Quest no longer appears in the mode selector

title: Redesign header connection badge for multi-device
body:
## Summary
- Removes device-specific name from the header entirely
- Connected state shows `● N Relic(s)` count (generic, scales to multiple devices)
- `+ Add` button bonds additional devices without navigating away
- `Disconnect` button disconnects the primary device (will become "Disconnect all" or move to RelicDrawer when multi-device is fully built out)
- Idle/connecting states unchanged: "Connect Relic" / "Connecting…"
- Drops the `lastKnownName`-based "Reconnect [name]" label — device names belong in the RelicDrawer, not the header

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Warn on page refresh when a relic is connected
body:
## Summary
Registers a `beforeunload` handler while any relic is connected. Attempting to refresh or close the tab triggers the browser's native "Leave site?" confirmation dialog. Handler is removed automatically when all devices disconnect.

## Note on custom text
Modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) intentionally ignore any custom message set on `beforeunload` for anti-phishing reasons — they always show their own generic prompt. This is standard browser behaviour and cannot be overridden.

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect a relic, press Ctrl+R / Cmd+R — browser should show "Leave site?" dialog
- [ ] Click "Stay" — page stays, device remains connected
- [ ] Click "Leave" — page refreshes, "Reconnect [name]" appears in header
- [ ] Disconnect the relic, then try to refresh — no dialog should appear

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Auto-reconnect on unexpected BLE disconnect
body:
## Summary
When a relic disconnects unexpectedly (out of range, power cycle, etc.) the app now silently retries the connection without any user input.

**How it works:**
- `gattserverdisconnected` event fires → reconnect loop starts immediately
- Retries with backoff: 1 s, 2 s, 4 s, 8 s, 16 s, 30 s × 3 (~3 min total window)
- While retrying, status shows "Connecting…" in the header
- On success: device is fully restored, UI updates, syncToDevice() fires
- On all attempts exhausted: device appears disconnected, header shows "Reconnect [name]" button
- Explicit Disconnect button cancels the retry loop and clears the saved name

**Page-refresh reconnect:**
- Still relies on `navigator.bluetooth.getDevices()` for silent reconnect after refresh
- Falls back to "Reconnect [name]" header button when that API is unavailable
- Removed the full-screen overlay (replaced by the automatic reconnect for mid-session disconnects)

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect relic, power cycle or move out of range — verify status shows "Connecting…" and reconnects without touching the UI
- [ ] Click Disconnect — verify reconnect does NOT happen
- [ ] Power cycle multiple times — verify repeated reconnects work
- [ ] Refresh page — verify "Reconnect [name]" appears in header (one-tap restore)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add automatic reconnect overlay for known relics
body:
## Summary
- Adds `ReconnectOverlay` — a full-screen modal that appears automatically 1.5 s after page load when a previously paired relic is known but not yet connected
- Shows the device name with a pulsing rune animation, a large "Reconnect" button, and a "Dismiss" option
- Auto-hides the moment the connection is restored
- The 1.5 s delay gives the silent `getDevices()` path time to succeed first; if it does, the overlay never appears
- Adds `dismissReconnect()` to the BLE context to let the user clear the persisted device name

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect a relic, then refresh — overlay should appear after ~1.5 s with the device name
- [ ] Tap "Reconnect" — BT picker opens, select device, overlay dismisses automatically on connect
- [ ] Tap "Dismiss" — overlay disappears and does not reappear on next refresh
- [ ] If `getDevices()` becomes available — overlay should never appear (status goes to connecting immediately)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add one-tap reconnect fallback for BLE
body:
## Summary
- Saves the last connected device name to `localStorage` on every successful connect
- Clears it when the user explicitly disconnects (so the button doesn't reappear after intentional disconnects)
- When the page loads without a connection but a previous device is known, the header shows **"Reconnect [device name]"** instead of the generic "Connect Relic" button
- Tapping it opens the browser BT picker pre-filtered to the Relic service — user just selects their device

This is a reliable fallback when `navigator.bluetooth.getDevices()` is unavailable (which turned out to be the case per diagnostic logging in #18).

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect a relic — verify name is saved (check localStorage key `relic:lastDevice` in DevTools → Application)
- [ ] Refresh the page — header should show "Reconnect Magic Compass" (or whatever the device name is)
- [ ] Tap the button — BT picker opens, select device, connection restores
- [ ] Click Disconnect — refresh page — header should show generic "Connect Relic" (name was cleared)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Fix BLE auto-reconnect: add Permissions-Policy bluetooth header
body:
## Summary
- Adds `vercel.json` with `Permissions-Policy: bluetooth=*` header on all routes
- Without this header, Chrome does not expose `navigator.bluetooth.getDevices()`, which is what auto-reconnect uses to recover previously authorized devices on page refresh
- Confirmed via diagnostic logging from PR #18: `[BLE] getDevices() not available in this browser`

## Test plan
- [ ] Deploy to Vercel
- [ ] Connect a compass relic manually
- [ ] Refresh the page — device should reconnect automatically within a few seconds
- [ ] Check console: should see `[BLE] getDevices() → 1 device(s)` followed by `[BLE] auto-reconnect: ... connected ✓`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add BLE auto-reconnect diagnostic logging
body:
## Summary
- Adds `[BLE]` console logging to `getKnownRelics()` and the `autoReconnect` loop
- Logs whether `getDevices()` is available, how many devices it returns, and the result of each connection attempt
- This is a diagnostic build to identify why auto-reconnect isn't working — no behavior change

## Test plan
- [ ] Connect a device manually, then refresh the page
- [ ] Open Chrome DevTools → Console, filter by `[BLE]`
- [ ] Report what log lines appear (especially whether `getDevices()` returns 0 or 1 device)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Fix auto-reconnect: connecting state, delay, and retry with backoff
body:
## Summary

The previous auto-reconnect attempt failed silently and had no retry — if the device needed even a moment to restart advertising after the old GATT connection dropped on page refresh, we gave up immediately with no UI feedback.

- Show `connecting` state as soon as known relics are detected, so the header shows "Connecting..." during the attempt
- Wait 800 ms before the first connect attempt (device needs to tear down its previous GATT connection and resume advertising)
- Retry up to 4 times per device with 2 s / 4 s / 6 s backoff between attempts
- Clear connecting state when done (whether successful or exhausted)

## Test plan

- [ ] Refresh page with a device in range — header shows "Connecting..." then transitions to connected within a few seconds
- [ ] Refresh with device temporarily out of range — retries are visible in header, eventually falls back to "Connect Relic"
- [ ] Manual connect button still works normally after failed auto-reconnect

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Sync compass state to device on connect/reconnect
body:
## Summary

- On connect (and auto-reconnect after page refresh), immediately push all current panel settings to the device so firmware matches the UI
- Covers: LEDs on/off, color, all-LEDs, spill, spin direction, speed, bearing, and mode
- Calibrate mode restores manual state on reconnect rather than re-triggering calibration

## How it works

`CompassControlPanel` tracks the previous value of `connected` via a ref. When `connected` flips `false → true` — whether from initial mount with a device already present (drawer) or from a reconnect (Compass page) — it calls `syncToDevice()` which fires all current settings via `sendFast`.

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a relic, change mode to Manual + Spin On, refresh — device resumes spin mode automatically after reconnect
- [ ] Set a color, disconnect and reconnect — color is restored on the device
- [ ] LEDs Off persists across reconnect
- [ ] Opening the relic drawer and connecting a second device immediately puts it in the current panel state

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Auto-reconnect previously authorized relics on page load
body:
## Summary

- On page load, silently attempt to reconnect any relics the browser has previously been granted permission to access — no user gesture required
- Uses `navigator.bluetooth.getDevices()` (Chrome 85+); gracefully falls back to idle on unsupported browsers (Firefox, Safari)
- Extracts `connectBleDevice()` in `useBle` as shared wiring used by both the user-triggered connect button and the auto-reconnect path

## How it works

1. `getKnownRelics()` in `BleDevice.ts` calls `getDevices()` and wraps results in `BleDevice` instances
2. A `useEffect` on mount iterates known devices and calls `connectBleDevice()` for each, silently swallowing errors for devices that are out of range
3. The user-triggered `connect()` still calls `requestDevice()` as before — no change to that flow

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a relic, refresh the page — device reconnects automatically within a few seconds
- [ ] Multiple relics reconnect if all were connected before refresh
- [ ] Out-of-range device at startup does not show an error — stays idle
- [ ] Manual "Connect Relic" button still works normally

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Compass controls: LEDs toggle, spin direction, spin-pulse mode
body:
## Summary

- Add LEDs On/Off toggle (always visible, works regardless of mode)
- Redesign Manual mode: replace Effect buttons with inline Spin and Pulse sub-controls
- Spin: direction (CW/CCW) and speed; Pulse: speed; both can be active simultaneously
- Controls always visible so settings can be pre-configured before enabling
- Add `compass.setLeds`, `compass.setSpinDirection`, and `"spin-pulse"` mode to BLE protocol
- Firmware implementation is in the TabletopRelics-Firmware repo (committed to main)

## Test plan

- [ ] LEDs On/Off toggle turns the ring off and restores it when turned back on
- [ ] Manual mode shows Bearing, All LEDs, Spill, Spin section, Pulse section, Color
- [ ] Spin On sends `spin` mode + direction + speed to device; CW/CCW changes direction
- [ ] Pulse On sends `pulse` mode + speed to device
- [ ] Both Spin and Pulse On sends `spin-pulse` mode
- [ ] Adjusting speed sliders while toggle is off does not send to device
- [ ] Turning toggle on picks up current slider values immediately

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Adventure action buttons, multi-device drawer, keep-open-after-connect
body:
## Summary

### Inline action buttons in adventure text
- `action:` links in adventure markdown render as tap-to-fire pill buttons instead of hyperlinks
- Buttons target the correct device type automatically (compass action → compass device)
- Dimmed/disabled when no matching device is connected; brief flash confirmation on fire
- Fixed react-markdown's default protocol sanitizer stripping `action:` URLs via `urlTransform`
- **The Shrouded Cairn** annotated with 10 action buttons at every key compass moment:
  - Discovery → ambient mode
  - Scene 1 arrival → faint pulse + slow speed
  - Scene 2 descent → quest mode pointing down the passage
  - Reliquary → amber pulse
  - Trade outcomes → off / ambient per choice
  - Aftermath → gold glow bonding moment

### Multi-device drawer
- `useBle` now manages `devices: DeviceView[]` — multiple relics can be connected simultaneously
- `+ Relic` button in drawer header bonds additional devices without closing the panel
- Device tabs appear automatically when 2+ relics are bonded; each tab shows its own controls
- Per-device Disconnect button in the drawer body
- Drawer **stays open after connecting** — shows controls immediately once bonded
- All existing pages and components unchanged via backward-compat shortcuts

### CompassControlPanel
- Now accepts `connected`/`send`/`sendFast` as props so the drawer can target a specific device

## Test plan

- [ ] Open The Shrouded Cairn — action buttons visible inline in narrative (dimmed, no device)
- [ ] With compass connected — buttons light up; tapping fires the BLE command
- [ ] Open drawer → tap "+ Relic" → browser device picker → select second device → drawer stays open, tabs appear
- [ ] Switch between device tabs — controls update to target the selected device
- [ ] Per-device Disconnect button works independently
- [ ] Connect a device from drawer empty state → drawer stays open and shows controls

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add floating relic drawer to adventure reading pages
body:
## Summary

- **CompassControlPanel**: Extracted all compass control state and UI from `Compass.tsx` into a standalone component, ready to embed anywhere without duplicating logic
- **RelicDrawer**: New right-edge FAB tab + slide-in drawer that appears on all adventure detail pages
  - A vertical pill tab sits flush to the right edge showing a status dot + "RELIC" label (or device name when connected)
  - Tapping/clicking slides in a 320px drawer from the right with a blurred backdrop
  - Connected compass → shows the full compass control panel inline
  - No device → shows a connect prompt
  - Future device types → generic fallback with a link to full controls
  - Footer link navigates to the full device controls page
- **Compass.tsx**: Simplified to use `CompassControlPanel`, removing ~200 lines of duplication

## Test plan

- [ ] Navigate to any adventure (`/adventures/:id`) — right-edge tab visible
- [ ] Tap tab → drawer slides in from right with blurred backdrop
- [ ] Tap backdrop or × → drawer closes
- [ ] With compass connected: drawer shows all compass controls (mode, effects, color, bearing, etc.)
- [ ] With no device: drawer shows "Bond a relic" prompt with Connect button
- [ ] "Open full controls →" link in drawer footer navigates to `/compass`
- [ ] Tab not visible on Home, Compass, Store, etc. (only on adventure pages)
- [ ] Compass page still works normally with its own control panel

title: Relocate battery indicator and unlock compass controls for exploration
body:
## Summary

- **Header**: Battery indicator removed from the connection badge
- **Home / connected device card**: Battery icon + % is now inline on the same row as relic type and firmware (between device name and firmware text)
- **Compass page**: Battery moves to just below the device name (`h1`), before the descriptive paragraph; only shown when connected
- **Mode buttons unlocked**: Ambient / Manual / Quest / Calibrate buttons and all manual-mode sub-controls (effect, bearing, spill, speed, color, all-LEDs) are now always interactive — users can explore the UI without a connected device; BLE commands are silently skipped when disconnected. The "Send target" button in Quest mode remains gated since it's an explicit send action.

## Test plan

- [ ] Header shows no battery — just device name + Disconnect button
- [ ] Home connected card shows `[icon] 72% · Magic Compass · firmware 1.2` on one line under the device name
- [ ] Compass page: battery appears between h1 and description when connected
- [ ] Without a device connected, all mode and effect buttons are clickable and update UI state
- [ ] Connecting a device after exploring sends the correct current mode on next command

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Fix battery display, update detection, and refresh button
body:
## Summary

- **Battery always visible**: `BatteryIcon` now accepts `percent: number | null` and renders a dimmed outline with a `?` when the value hasn't arrived yet. All three locations (header badge, Home device card, Compass page) always show the widget once connected, displaying `–` until a value is known.
- **Battery listener race fixed**: `BleDevice` caches the last-read battery value in `lastBattery` and immediately replays it to any `onBattery()` subscriber that registers after `connect()` returns — the root cause of the "never shows %" bug.
- **Update banner on mobile**: `onRegisteredSW` callback now calls `registration.update()` every 60 s and on every `visibilitychange` event, so the banner fires when a mobile user returns to a backgrounded tab.
- **Refresh button actually refreshes**: After calling `updateServiceWorker(true)`, a 300 ms `setTimeout` calls `window.location.reload()` as a guaranteed fallback so the page always reloads.

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a device — battery % and icon appear in the header immediately (no second connect needed)
- [ ] Disconnect / reconnect — battery shows on reconnect without requiring a page reload
- [ ] When battery is unavailable, header shows `–` with a faint outline icon instead of hiding the widget
- [ ] Update banner: deploy a new build, then background and foreground the tab on mobile — banner appears
- [ ] Clicking Refresh reloads the page and banner disappears

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Fix battery always showing on connect
body:
## Summary

Battery % was only populated when the firmware sent a BLE notification (every 30 s), so it would show up late or not at all. Now `BleDevice.connect()` explicitly reads the battery characteristic value immediately after subscribing and fires all listeners right away — so the display is always populated the moment a device connects.

Firmware companion (`TabletopRelics-Firmware` commit `39e1822`):
- Fixed pulse mode bearing slider bug: `setTarget` was switching mode to quest for any non-manual mode, which killed pulse. Now pulse is also exempted.
- `showSpinStep` now accepts `spillCount` — spin uses the same 12.5%-per-level spill falloff as static/pulse.

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a device — battery % appears immediately in header, Home card, and Compass page
- [ ] Visible on mobile layout
- [ ] Pulse mode: drag bearing slider — LED point moves but pulse animation continues
- [ ] Spin mode with spill > 0: trailing LEDs dim correctly
- [ ] Vercel build passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Battery on Home card + Compass page, shared BatteryIcon
body:
## Summary

- **Home connected device card**: battery icon + % + "Charging" label shown below the device name and firmware version
- **Compass control page**: same battery row below the page description
- **BatteryIcon extracted** to `src/components/BatteryIcon.tsx` — shared across ConnectionBadge, Home, and Compass; no duplicate code

Firmware: spill falloff steepened to 12.5% per level (`TabletopRelics-Firmware` commit `ddc2fc1`).

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a device — battery appears in header, Home card, and Compass page
- [ ] Charging label shows in all three locations when `charging: true`
- [ ] Disconnected state shows no battery anywhere
- [ ] Vercel build passes

title: Battery on mobile + update-available banner
body:
## Summary

- **Battery always visible**: icon + percentage now show on all screen sizes, not just `sm:`. Device name stays hidden on mobile to keep the header tidy. Amber "Charging" label appears alongside when the device reports USB power.
- **Update banner**: when a new deployment is detected via the PWA service worker, a bottom sheet appears with "A new version is available" and Refresh / Dismiss buttons. Uses `vite-plugin-pwa`'s `useRegisterSW` hook (`registerType` changed from `autoUpdate` to `prompt` so the user controls when to reload).

Firmware: spill falloff steepened to 25% per level (separate commit to `TabletopRelics-Firmware`, `6e2e117`).

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a relic on mobile — battery icon + % visible in header
- [ ] Charging label appears when `charging: true` in state
- [ ] Battery color: green >60%, amber >30%, red ≤30%
- [ ] Deploy a second time — update banner appears on the existing open tab
- [ ] Refresh button reloads to new version; Dismiss hides the banner
- [ ] Vercel build passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Battery icon + charging indicator in header
body:
## Summary

- Header now shows a battery icon (SVG) next to the device name when connected
- Fill color is green (>60%), amber (>30%), or red (≤30%) based on percent
- A white lightning bolt overlays the icon when the device reports `charging: true`
- Battery percentage shown as text alongside the icon
- `charging?: boolean` added to all `RelicState` variants in `protocol.ts`

Firmware companion (`TabletopRelics-Firmware` commit `b8dc70f`):
- Reads actual battery voltage from GPIO 35 (A13, VBAT/2 on HUZZAH32 v2)
- Notifies BLE client every 30 s with updated percent
- Sends `charging` field in state JSON (detected via `PIN_CHARGE_STAT` if defined in `CompassConfig.h`, otherwise `false`)

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect a relic — device name, green battery icon, and percent appear in header
- [ ] Battery icon turns amber at ≤60%, red at ≤30%
- [ ] Charging bolt appears when firmware sends `charging: true`
- [ ] Disconnected state still shows "Connect Relic" button
- [ ] Vercel deploy passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add spill, all-LEDs, and bearing slider to pulse/static effects
body:
## Summary

- **Pulse mode** now keeps its bearing slider (labelled "Active LED") alongside the speed control
- **All LEDs toggle** — when on, every compass point lights up (or pulses) together; bearing and spill grey out
- **Spill slider (0–4)** — lights N neighboring LEDs at diminishing brightness (50%, 33%, 25%…) for a softer glow; visible on static and pulse when All LEDs is off
- Static effect gets the same All LEDs and Spill controls

Firmware companion: `TabletopRelics-Firmware` commit `ca38839` adds `showPointWithSpill`, `showAllPoints`, updated `showPulseStep` with phaseStep/allLeds/spillCount, decoupled pulse timer, and `setSpill`/`setAll` command handlers.

## Test plan

- [ ] Static effect: drag bearing slider → single LED moves; increase spill → neighbors light up; enable All LEDs → all 8 points on, bearing/spill grey out
- [ ] Pulse effect: single point breathes; bearing slider selects which point; spill adds soft neighbors; All LEDs pulses entire ring; speed slider changes pulse rate
- [ ] Spin / Random: no bearing, no spill, no All LEDs — only speed and color
- [ ] Switching between effects clears previous state cleanly
- [ ] Vercel deploy passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Fix TS build errors in Compass page
body:
Remove two unused variables (`activeMode`, `firmwareMode`) that broke the TypeScript build on Vercel.

title: Add spin/pulse/random effects with speed control and fast BLE writes
body:
## Summary

- **3 new light effects** within manual mode: Spin (chasing LED), Pulse (breathing sine wave), Random (random point flashes)
- **Speed slider** (Slow → Fast, 1–100) for all animated effects
- **Fast BLE writes** — slider and colour changes now use `writeValueWithoutResponse`, eliminating the ACK round-trip for real-time responsiveness
- **🎲 Random colour** toggle — each animation step picks a vivid colour from a palette

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect compass, switch to Manual → effect buttons (Static/Spin/Pulse/Random) appear
- [ ] Static: bearing slider updates LED in real-time with no perceptible lag
- [ ] Spin: LED chases around ring, speed slider controls rate
- [ ] Pulse: LED at 0° breathes in/out, speed slider controls rate
- [ ] Random: random LED flashes, speed slider controls rate
- [ ] 🎲 Random colour: colour picker dims, effects cycle through vivid palette
- [ ] Colour swatches update LED immediately on tap

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Compass manual mode + BLE protocol fixes
body:
## Summary

- **Protocol fixes**: aligned UUIDs, command field (`op` not `cmd`), command names, and state field names (`heading`/`target`) between firmware and web app so the device actually connects and communicates
- **Manual mode**: new compass mode with a real-time bearing slider (40ms debounce) and color picker — 5 presets (blue, gold, green, violet, red) plus a free-form color input; dial target tick tints to match the selected color
- **Mode selector**: replaced scattered buttons with a clean Ambient / Manual / Quest / Calibrate row

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect Magic Compass via "Connect Relic" in Chrome — device should appear as "Magic Compass" in the picker
- [ ] Verify green chase animation on connect, badge shows device name
- [ ] Switch to **Manual** mode — color picker and live slider appear
- [ ] Drag slider — ring LED follows in real-time
- [ ] Tap a color swatch — LED color changes immediately
- [ ] Switch to **Quest** mode — "Send target" button appears, slider no longer auto-sends
- [ ] Switch to **Ambient** — compass reverts to sensor-driven north (or fault blink if sensor disabled)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add product tier selector (Finished / Kit / DIY)

- New `/store/:productId` detail page with hero, interactive tier selector, and contextual CTA (pre-order form for paid tiers, GitHub links for DIY)
